meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve can't debug code (Advanced)
content.seo_description: Learn how to optimize SaaS pricing and overcome debugging hurdles. An Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code to build your first project.
content.answer_block: Debug complex SaaS pricing logic by automating your feedback loops. Use Antigravity to map pricing edge cases and Claude Code to refactor your implementation. By treating pricing as a functional state machine rather than hardcoded logic, you eliminate hidden bugs and create a scalable, predictable revenue architecture for your app.
content.intro:
You are staring at a screen of red text, the cursor blinking in a rhythm that feels like a mockery of your progress. You have built the core features, but the pricing logic—the lifeblood of your SaaS—is a tangled web of unhandled edge cases and silent failures that your traditional debugger cannot resolve. The frustration isn't just about syntax; it is about the paralyzing fear that your foundation is fundamentally broken.

But here is the truth: debugging isn't just about finding errors; it is about mastering the architecture of your intent. When you combine the structural precision of Antigravity with the deep reasoning capabilities of Claude Code, you transform from a struggling student into an architect of robust systems. This guide bridges the gap between chaotic code and a production-ready, revenue-generating machine.

steps.title: Mapping the Pricing State Machine
steps.description: Before writing code, map out every pricing transition (free, trial, premium, enterprise) as a state machine to visualize where logic breaks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current pricing architecture and define a state machine diagram that handles subscription transitions, trial expirations, and upgrade/downgrade logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create --file pricing_logic.ts --context 'Define a state machine for user subscription tiers'

steps.title: Automating Test Case Generation
steps.description: Generate edge-case scenarios for your pricing engine, specifically focusing on failed payments and mid-cycle tier changes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of unit tests for my pricing logic that covers failed payments, mid-cycle upgrades, and prorated billing calculations.

steps.title: Refactoring for Idempotency
steps.description: Ensure your pricing updates are idempotent so that retrying a failed request doesn't result in double-billing or corrupted user state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor my pricing service to ensure all external API calls for billing are idempotent and handle race conditions.

steps.title: Implementing Observability Hooks
steps.description: Inject logging hooks into your pricing flow to catch silent failures before they impact revenue metrics.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add structured logging to my pricing engine that captures input, output, and error states for debugging production issues.

steps.title: Validating Against Sandbox Environments
steps.description: Use Claude Code to simulate real-time API responses from Stripe or Paddle to verify your pricing logic handles network latency.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Simulate network latency and API timeout scenarios for my subscription checkout flow to ensure graceful failure.

pro_tips: Always model your pricing logic as a pure function; if it relies on external state, you will never be able to debug it reliably.
Use 'Time-Travel Debugging' logs to capture the state of the user object at every transition point during your pricing logic execution.
Never hardcode currency conversion rates; fetch them at runtime via a cached service to avoid stale pricing errors.
Implement a 'dry-run' mode in your CLI tools that allows you to simulate a user's entire billing lifecycle without touching real database records.
Treat your pricing tests as documentation; if a developer cannot understand your billing logic by reading the tests, the logic is too complex.
content.next_steps: Deploy your validated pricing engine to a staging environment, run a series of simulated transactions using Claude Code, and then perform a 'canary' release to a small set of beta users to observe logs in real-time.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your code and start building the future of your SaaS with an engineered approach that guarantees reliability and revenue growth. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

Ensure your pricing updates are idempotent so that retrying a failed request doesn't result in double-billing or corrupted user state.
